checks.a_analysis: The output content was cross-referenced with the source PDF. All headings (Scope, Material Superseded, Responsibilities, PE-1 through PE-20, Enforcement) and their associated body text accurately reflect the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. AI-generated elements like table summaries and captions are explicitly permitted.

No contact information such as phone numbers, emails, or physical addresses is present in the source PDF. The document references (e.g., SCIO-SEC-313, N.C.G.S. §143B-1376) were checked and found to be either correctly transcribed or omitted (in the case of Document No in the header). No fabricated contact information was introduced.

The output JSON contains 12 pages worth of content. All major sections (PE-1 to PE-20) and the final Enforcement section are present in the output. The reading order and logical flow from the 12-page source document are maintained.

The 'Roles and Definitions' table spans pages 1 and 2 of the source. It contains 4 data rows (Senior Management, Security Liaison, Facility Management, Third Parties) plus a header row. The output JSON table correctly captures all 5 rows (1 header + 4 data).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Each policy control section (PE-1 to PE-20) was examined for internal completeness. Sub-points (e.g., PE-2 a-f, PE-3 a-k) and nested sub-lists (e.g., PE-8 a i-ix) were verified. All substantial text content from within these sections is preserved in the output.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output document fails to include critical document metadata found in the header of every page of the source PDF. This includes the 'Document No. (SCIO-SEC-313)', 'Effective Date (01/29/2018)', 'Review Date (03/26/2025)', and 'Version (4)'. While the body text of the policy is accurate, the omission of these identifying dates and version numbers is a defect for a government policy document where auditability is key.
checks.b_evidence_source: Document No. SCIO-SEC-313, Effective Date 01/29/2018, Review Date 03/26/2025, Version 4

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON correctly handles the source document's recurring headers (document title and metadata on every page). It extracts the title once as a heading and filters out the repeated footer/header information. No content from the main body, lists, or tables appears more than once in the output.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. The primary sections (Scope, Material Superseded, Responsibilities, PE-1 through PE-20, Enforcement) are all rendered as H2 headings. There are no skipped levels or incorrectly nested headings. While the pipeline divided some PE sections into separate JSON section objects, the hierarchy within those objects remains logical.

The table in the 'Responsibilities' section, which spans Page 1 and Page 2 of the source PDF, is successfully reconstructed as a single table in the JSON. The header row is correctly identified, and the data cells for 'Third Parties' (from Page 2) are accurately appended to the table structure started on Page 1.

The reading order of the JSON output follows the sequential flow of the source PDF. Information is presented from the initial scope through the various policy controls and ends with the enforcement section, matching the original layout.

In section PE-17 (Alternate Work Site), the list structure is corrupted. Sub-items 'ii' through 'vi' (which are part of the nested list under item 'd' on Page 12) are incorrectly extracted as top-level sibling items to 'd'. In the source, item 'd' begins on Page 11 and its sub-items continue onto Page 12. The output fails to preserve this nesting across the page break.
checks.b_evidence_source: d. Alternate work sites must meet state and federal security control requirements... The following shall be implemented for alternate work sites: i. Sufficient physical access... ii. Design requirements... iii. Equipment being used... iv. Equipment transported... v. Equipment must not... vi. NIST SP 800-46...
checks.b_evidence_output: { "text": "d. Alternate work sites must meet state and federal security control requirements...", "nested_list": { "items": [ { "content": [ { "text": "i. Sufficient physical access..." } ] } ] } }, { "content": [ { "text": "ii. Design requirements..." } ] }, { "content": [ { "text": "iii. Equipment being used..." } ] }
checks.b_evidence_location: PE-17 – Alternate Work Site, Page 11 and 12

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains references to various policies and standards (e.g., NIST SP 800-53, SCIO-SEC-313) but no active hyperlinks. The output faithfully represents these as plain text without injecting fabricated links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the same logical flow and single-column layout as the source PDF. Sections are presented in the correct order. The repeating header information from the PDF is handled by injecting elements (seal, title) into the flow at page breaks, which while redundant, does not constitute a major layout failure that confuses meaning.

The 'Roles and Definitions for Information Security' table is correctly rendered. It successfully merges the 'Third Parties' row (which was on page 2 in the source) into the main table body. Columns are aligned, headers are distinct, and the content is perfectly readable.
The primary meaningful image, the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina, is present in the HTML output and rendered correctly multiple times.

Content from the page headers in the source PDF (specifically the NC State Seal and the policy title) is being duplicated and injected into the body of the HTML document at what would be page breaks. For example, the seal and the title 'Physical and Environmental Protection Policy' appear again just before the PE-1 section, and the seal appears again before PE-3. These elements should only appear once at the top of the document in a continuous HTML flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: The Great Seal of the State of North Carolina and 'Physical and Environmental Protection Policy' text appear in the header of every page (e.g., Page 2, Page 4).
checks.b_evidence_output: The NC State Seal and the title 'Physical and Environmental Protection Policy' are repeated between the 'Responsibilities' table and 'PE-1'. The seal is repeated again before 'PE-3'.
checks.b_evidence_location: In HTML output, preceding section headers for PE-1 and PE-3.
